transcript: Crime Desk. What can I do for ya?
Arthur, my name is Ruby and I understand you wanna make a donation to the Jewish War Veterans?
You know, I don’t know where you people are gettin’ this crap from. I don’t know where you’re gettin’ our telephone, 'cause this is a news desk—a Crime News Desk.
Sir, whoever put you on the phone was— called us, we did not call you, sir. Or whoever the gentleman was that transfer the call to you, he was the one that put you on the phone.
Now this is the second telephone call I got from you damn people, so lay off!—
Sir… Beg your pardon! You do not curse at me!—
Hey, I don’t need pardon. This is a police line, you stupid bitch!
Avery told me to contact you.
And wha—? And what—what is your full name?
How do you spell that last name?
Oh, what did, uh… Avery tell you to contact me about?
That you wanted to give a donation to the Polish Legion American Veterans.
To—? [laughs] Oh I…’m not workin’ right now, so I can’t do any donations.
Okay, that’s alright with me. Just, uh…— Well tell Avery not to… give his money away like that.
What can I do for ya?
I was actually just transferred to the phone number. Uh, someone wanted to make it a donation to the National Coalition for Homeless Veterans.
I don’t donate to none o’ those crooked companies.
Someone just sent me to this number. They told me to ask for Arthur.
Hell, I’m a federal police news reporter and this is one o’ their hotlines [inhales] and we’re tryin’ to still find out who in the hell’s been givin’ out our number.
Well this is a… nonprofit organization, so it wouldn’t be us.
Hi, can I speak to Travis?
Hi, Travis. I’m calling from the National Amputation Foundation. I just received a phone call from Mr. Barnes? [unclear]—
This is the National Amputation Foundation. I was just transferred to you. He said that you were looking to send a cash donation to the Organization? You don’t know what I’m talking about?

Caller I.D. said "A-T-T" on it. I don’t know. He said that you had asked him to find the name of the organization that you wanted to send a donation to. He did. He called us—he got us—and then he transferred me to you!
It’s the National Amputation Foundation. It’s a veteran’s organization.
I got nothin’ to do with that, I’m sorry.
Alright, that’s not a problem. Bye-bye.
Hi, sir. My name is Lucy from the Military Officers Association of America. I just spoke with your son and he said that you’d been trying to reach us about making a donation to M.O.A.A.?
If you talked to my son, he would’ve told you to go to hell.
Hello, good evening, how may I help you?
Uh… I didn’t know. Uh… Why— why you calling?

Uh… No, I’m not calling anybody, sir. I— Actually, I just got a call from one of your colleagues, uh, ya know, regarding some foundations for the Club. But I just wanted to inform you that you have to call back in the morning and speak-a to a member of the… management.
[unclear] That’s an army club, sir. That’s a military club. That is the Cavalry and Guards Club.—
I see. I got it. But— But I… I.. I’m not aware about any Club and, uh… it’s some kind of mistake.
Well, sir, I don’t know because I’m just saying that [unclear]—
Oh, no, I understand, uh… that, uh…—
I just received a call from your friend.—
But— But— I— I— I got— I got a call, uh… I think, uh-uh, from, uh-uh—uh-uh— this number before as well, so… so— But I don’t know if you can help me to take me out of the list of [chuckles] numbers.

But, uh, well, uh, you know, it’s nothing really I can say, because I just have a phone call from somebody, sir. And they say that that "We’ll put you through to speak to my colleague". So, um, I’m just wanna… give you exactly the same answer I give to your colleague. Any matters, you know, regarding what your colleague talking about—because I’m getting very confused at the moment—is: you have to call the office in the morning and you speak to Sergeant Machau. [unclear] Calvary.—
Uh, hello, this is Selma. I’m from the Women’s Memorial? I understand that you were trying to contact us? [unclear]—

What the hell am I calling dead women’s clubs for?! And I don’t know where you’re gettin’ this telephone number from! I’m a federal police news reporter and you’re… comin’ in on my police line, here! You women are nuts!—
Hi. I just had someone named Mr. Barnes call me and tell me that you wanna make a donation of some kind?
Uh… [laughs] A donation? Okay. Tell me about it.
That you wanted to make a donation.
To our Foundation for the scholarship fund.
American Military Retirees Association. Do you know who Mr. Barnes is? [unclear] [laughs]
Uh, we’re not quite sure what’s going on, here.
Sounds like one of those "punk" things. [laughs]
I— If Mr. Barnes is the guy that I think he is, he would never have done that. So, uh, nice try, asshole.
Yes? This is Lamita Port—the gateway station—Fort Latuka [?]. And who am I speaking to?
[stammers] I— I— I— Okay, I’m not sure… I got, uh, who… who is calling.
You wanted to talk to somebody in the radio room?
Uh, I— I— I have no clue.
Uh… I’m not sure who is this [unclear] but I cannot, uh… cannot help, uh, anything.
Does this have to do with any kinda radio communication?
No, I— I have no idea.
O…kay. Are you trying to get ahold of a MARS station?
I don’t call myself a "MARS station". I am the Mars Gateway.
No, it’s something wrong with this phone, because, uh, I already getting this phone calls and—
I know! This is the second time!
I know. I’m having the same problem. I’m talkin’ to someone else and then all of a sudden you come into the phone conversation.
Ugh, no, I mean, I have no intentions…
Oh, I know you don’t, sir! I have no intentions of talkin’ to you, either.
Uh, so I’m thinking of doing a complain about this. 'Cause, have you any idea, you know…?
I have no idea! Did you pick up or something.
No, I— I just getting a… ring and I’m picking up the phone and that’s it. I mean, and I see your number, I mean… you don’t know what to do.—
I— I know, but I didn’t call.
You don’t wanna give your name, don’t use my fuckin’ number!
[Skype hangup tone][ Arthur Schaffner exits call]
Sir, I’ve talked to three people on your conversation. Whadda you need to know about the Mars program, sir?
One-zero-eight [redacted], what the hell’s wrong!? You don’t give your fuckin’ name!
We don’t know what’s going on! For some reason, three phone calls are interacting right now. We have no clue right now. We’re trying to fix it, sir.
[emergency sirens][ringing tone][ Arthur Schaffner enters call]
I’m just gonna try calling you back, so hang up, sir.
